name: Spicy Beef Back Ribs
recipeIngredient: 5 pounds beef back ribs, cut into 4 sections
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, combine the catsup, water, grated onion, lemon juice, hot pepper sauce, and crushed red pepper pods.
2. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat.
3. Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally until warm.
4. Prepare the grill for indirect cooking (see notes).
5. Place the beef back ribs on the grid centered over the drip pan with the meat side up.
6. Cover the cooker and grill the ribs for 45 to 60 minutes or until they are tender, turning them occasionally.
7. Brush the reserved sauce over the ribs and continue grilling, covered, for an additional 10 minutes.
8. To prepare the grill for indirect cooking, arrange an equal amount of briquets on each side of the grill.
9. Place an aluminum foil drip pan in the center between the coals.
10. Allow the coals to ash over approximately 30 minutes until they are burning evenly on both sides.
11. Remove the ribs from the packets and place them back on the grid.
12. Continue grilling for an additional 10 to 20 minutes, turning once, until the internal temperature reaches your desired level (cautiously hold your hand about 4 inches above the coals; low to medium heat will cause it to be removed in 4 to 5 seconds).
